questions.question: Which preprocessor directive is used to include the iostream header file?
questions.explanation: The #include <iostream> directive tells the preprocessor to include the iostream header, which provides cout and cin for input/output.

questions.question: What is the correct way to output the text "Hello" to the screen in C++?
questions.explanation: In C++, cout (character output) with the stream insertion operator << is used to display output to the screen.

questions.question: Which data type would you use to store a number with a decimal point, such as 3.14?
questions.explanation: The double data type stores floating-point numbers (numbers with decimal points). int stores whole numbers, char stores single characters, and string stores text.
When both operands are integers, C++ performs integer division, which truncates the decimal portion. 10 / 3 = 3 (not 3.33).

questions.question: Every C++ program must have a function called ___.
questions.explanation: The main() function is the entry point of every C++ program. Execution begins at the first statement inside main().

questions.question: The ___ object is used to read input from the keyboard in C++.
questions.explanation: cin (character input) with the stream extraction operator >> reads data from the keyboard and stores it in a variable.
